name: AVPV/PeN kisspeptin neuron
anatomy_mentioned: rostral periventricular area of the third ventricle (RP3V)
nt_type: GABAergic/dopaminergic (most AVPV/PeN Kiss1 cells co-express tyrosine hydroxylase)
morphology_summary: Female-biased sexually dimorphic population: females have a greater number of Kiss1 neurons in the AVPV than males; sexual dimorphism established during the perinatal critical period through testosterone exposure. Most AVPV/PeN Kiss1 cells co-express TH (the rate-limiting enzyme in dopamine synthesis). AVPV is larger in volume, contains more cells, and sends more projections to multiple reproduction-related brain regions in females than males.
ephys_summary: Bimodal resting membrane potential influenced by KATP channels, with two populations: type I (irregular firing) and type II (quiescent). AVPV/PeN Kiss1 neurons display sexually dimorphic biophysical properties: male neurons exhibit higher action-potential frequency and receive more excitatory synaptic transmission (higher sEPSC frequency) compared to females.
subtypes_noted: Two electrophysiological subtypes (type I irregular firing, type II quiescent). Distinct from arcuate Kiss1 population which is not sexually dimorphic. Mediates estrogen-positive feedback for the preovulatory GnRH/LH surge in females.

name: Arcuate KNDy (kisspeptin/neurokinin B/dynorphin) neuron
morphology_summary: Located in the arcuate nucleus; not sexually dimorphic in cell number or Kiss1 mRNA distribution between sexes. Generates pulsatile GnRH-mediated gonadotropin secretion in both sexes through inhibition by gonadal hormones. Receives sexually dimorphic afferent input despite monomorphic distribution: female KNDy cells receive a higher percentage of afferents from the rostral periventricular area (RP3V/PeN), with a greater fraction of those afferents co-expressing ERalpha in females.
ephys_summary: Heterogeneous pattern of cellular activity (firing and quiescent cells), but no bimodal RMP distribution. No sexually dimorphic pattern of cellular activity in the ARC, in line with the lack of sex difference in Kiss1 mRNA distribution.
subtypes_noted: Distinct from AVPV/PeN Kiss1 population (which is dimorphic). KNDy cells co-express kisspeptin, neurokinin B, and dynorphin.

name: AVPV tyrosine hydroxylase (TH) neuron
nt_type: dopaminergic (TH is rate-limiting enzyme for dopamine synthesis)
morphology_summary: Female-biased sexually dimorphic TH-immunoreactive population: females have 2-4 times more TH-positive neurons than males in the AVPV, with size differences in addition to number. The AVPV consists mainly of TH-positive neurons. Ovariectomy does not affect TH expression; chronic estradiol reduces it. TH-ir lesioned females show lower maternal behavior, suggesting a role in sexually dimorphic parental behavior.
ephys_summary: Not described in detail in the report (overlap with Kiss1 population shares biophysical features described above).
subtypes_noted: Substantial overlap with AVPV/PeN Kiss1 neurons (most AVPV/PeN Kiss1 cells co-express TH). Distinct from SDN-POA where TH-positive cell bodies are absent (only TH-ir axons/synapses).

name: Sexually dimorphic nucleus of the preoptic area (SDN-POA) calbindin neuron
anatomy_mentioned: sexually dimorphic nucleus of the preoptic area (SDN-POA)
interstitial nucleus of the anterior hypothalamus (INAH) in humans
morphology_summary: Male-biased dimorphism: SDN-POA is larger in males than females and controls male sex behavior. Delineated histologically by Nissl staining or immunohistochemically by calbindin-D28K immunoreactivity. No TH-positive cell bodies are present, but fine TH-positive axon-like projections/synaptic structures are observed.
subtypes_noted: Considered the rodent homolog of the third interstitial nucleus of the anterior hypothalamus (INAH3) in humans, and homologous structures in other mammals (ovine SDN, rhesus medial preoptic area) and non-mammalian species (quail).

name: MPOA estrogen receptor 1 (Esr1) neuron
nt_type: not specified (heterogeneous; MPOA contains both GABAergic and glutamatergic populations)
morphology_summary: Highly sexually dimorphic in distribution and projection patterns. Highly expressed in MPOA neurons involved in both parental behaviors and male-type mating behaviors. The MPOA is larger and contains more neurons in males than females. Esr1+ population overlaps with steroid hormone receptor-expressing neurons (Ar, Pgr) enriched in MPOA, where differential effects of circulating steroids between sexes underlie sexually dimorphic features.
subtypes_noted: MPOA Esr1 neurons are required (alongside galanin neurons) for pup-directed/parental behavior. Esr1 governs male-type mating behavior; overlapping Nts (neurotensin)+ neurons govern female socio-sexual behaviors.

name: MPOA galanin neuron
morphology_summary: Sexually dimorphic MPOA subpopulation essential for parental behaviors. One of two MPOA subpopulations (with Esr1+) required for the regulation of pup-directed behavior.
subtypes_noted: Likely overlaps partially with MPOA Esr1+ population.

name: VMHvl estrogen-receptor alpha / progesterone receptor neuron
anatomy_mentioned: ventrolateral subdivision of the ventromedial hypothalamus (VMHvl)
nt_type: not specified (heterogeneous, includes glutamatergic VMH neurons)
morphology_summary: Sexually dimorphic population in VMHvl that controls sex-typical behaviors (mating in both sexes, fighting in males). Contains ERalpha+ neurons coordinating metabolism and reproduction in females; subset co-expressing ERalpha + Nkx2-1 + Tac1 promotes hormone-dependent female locomotion. snRNA-seq identified 17 transcriptomic types, several sexually dimorphic; VMHvl is essential for lordosis behavior in females through projections to PAG, brainstem, and spinal motor circuits.
ephys_summary: Not directly described in the report.
subtypes_noted: Multiple molecularly defined subpopulations: Pgr+ (mating in both sexes / fighting in males), ERalpha+/Nkx2-1+/Tac1+ (female locomotion), heterogeneous ERalpha-expressing subtypes with different projections and sex-related functions (Ngn3-dependent SF1 neurons during development).

name: PVN corticotropin-releasing factor receptor 1 (CRFR1) neuron
morphology_summary: Male-biased sexual dimorphism in PVN (males > females). Sex difference is not present at postnatal days 0, 4, or 21 — emerges during puberty/early adulthood and persists in old mice (20-24 months). Adult gonadectomy reduces CRFR1-immunoreactive cells in males but not females. Restraint stress induces sexually dimorphic neural activation (males > females) measured by phospho-CREB co-localization.
subtypes_noted: Co-express estrogen receptor alpha (moderate) and androgen receptor (high), suggesting gonadal-hormone-dependent regulation.

name: BNST (anterolateral) corticotropin-releasing factor (CRF) neuron
anatomy_mentioned: dorsolateral bed nucleus of the stria terminalis (dlBNST)
morphology_summary: Female-biased sexual dimorphism: females have larger and more numerous CRF neurons in the dorsolateral BNST than males. Sex difference not evident at postnatal day 5; emerges by adulthood. Ovariectomy reduces CRF neuron number in females; orchiectomy increases it in males. CRF neurons in dlBNST lack calbindin (contrast with calbindin+ principal nucleus of BNST). Modulates pain sensitivity via CRF release; type II dlBNST neurons (electrophysiological class) show CRF-induced excitability that is suppressed by NPY.
ephys_summary: CRF increases neuronal excitability specifically in 'type II' dlBNST neurons (whole-cell patch-clamp); NPY decreases excitability in the same type II neurons and suppresses CRF-induced excitation.
subtypes_noted: Distinguished by electrophysiological type II classification within dlBNST. Distinct from calbindin+ principal nucleus of BNST. Closely related to Kiss1-expressing populations in the principal nucleus (prBNST), which are male-biased.

name: BNST (principal nucleus) kisspeptin neuron
morphology_summary: Male-biased sexual dimorphism in prBNST: males have significantly more Kiss1prBNST neurons than females (139.4 +/- 23.32 vs. 43.70 +/- 5.33). This sex-biased distribution is absent in Kiss1aBNST neurons. prBNST Kiss1 neurons project to dlBNST, juxta-periventricular lateral hypothalamic area (JPLH), and posterolateral stria terminalis. aBNST Kiss1 neurons project to AVPV, MPO, LPO, and PVH.
subtypes_noted: Two BNST Kiss1 subpopulations: prBNST (male-biased) and aBNST (not sexually dimorphic), with distinct projection targets. Integrates HPG axis with sex-dependent social behaviors and metabolic functions.

name: Lateral septum (intermediate division) kisspeptin neuron
morphology_summary: Female-biased sexual dimorphism in ILS: females have significantly more Kiss1ILS neurons than males (74.40 +/- 17.68 vs. 7.00 +/- 2.864). This dimorphism is absent in Kiss1VLS neurons. Regulated by sex steroids; LS integrates social, feeding, anxiety, and fear behaviors.
subtypes_noted: Two LS Kiss1 subpopulations: ILS (female-biased) and VLS (not dimorphic).

name: Arcuate aromatase neuron
morphology_summary: Male-biased sexually dimorphic cluster of aromatase neurons in the arcuate nucleus, adjacent to kisspeptin neurons. Part of a network of approximately 6,000 aromatase neurons across hypothalamus and amygdala. Becomes sexually dimorphic by birth. Male arcuate aromatase neurons convert testosterone to estrogen locally to regulate kisspeptin neuron activity.
subtypes_noted: Part of a broader aromatase network spanning hypothalamus and amygdala.

name: POA PACAP/VIP hypophysiotropic neuron
morphology_summary: Single population of POA neurons projecting to the anterior pituitary that shows opposite sexually dimorphic neuropeptide expression: PACAP is male-biased while VIP is female-biased. Sexual dimorphism caused by adult estrogen secretion. Acts as hypophysiotropic factors via functional pituitary receptors.
subtypes_noted: Studied in medaka (teleost); single population with sex-dependent neuropeptide expression.

name: Ventral premammillary nucleus (PMv) oxytocin receptor neuron
morphology_summary: Male-biased sexual dimorphism: significantly higher OTR expression in males compared to females between P14 and P56. PMv is a well-established sexually dimorphic region with roles in maternal aggression, reproductive control, male social behavior, and intermale aggression. Hemminger et al. identified subclass-level abundance differences in PMv. Previously identified subpopulations include PMv-DAT and PMv-PACAP.
subtypes_noted: Multiple molecularly defined subpopulations within PMv: PMv-DAT (dopamine transporter+) and PMv-PACAP, in addition to OTR+.
